flotilla_bullets: Architected a distributed training framework in Python/AsyncIO orchestrating 1,000+ worker nodes, implementing both synchronous and asynchronous parameter aggregation strategies (FedAvg, FedAsync, tiered aggregation) via a pluggable event-driven state-machine design

Designed a stateful parameter-server architecture with stateless workers, externalizing all orchestration state to Redis for real-time durability, cutting per-round overhead to just 1.7% at 1,000+ nodes vs. 54.9% for a leading open-source baseline

Built gRPC + MQTT based worker discovery and heartbeat-driven liveness detection, and implemented server-side fault tolerance via incremental checkpointing and hot failover, recovering a live session on a standby server within 820ms with rollback limited to a single round

Validated the framework across heterogeneous accelerators (Nvidia Jetson GPUs, ARM edge devices) training CNN, LSTM, and transformer (ALBERT) architectures on PyTorch/HuggingFace, demonstrating tolerance to ~40% worker dropout with negligible accuracy impact

technical_skills: LLM & Distributed Training Infra: SGLang, NCCL, PyTorch, HuggingFace, Async/Sync Aggregation, Checkpoint/Restore, Fault-Tolerant Orchestration
Systems & Networking: Linux, Docker/Podman, CRIU, namespaces/cgroups, gRPC, MQTT, Reliable UDP, TCP/IP, REST APIs

selected_project.title: Distributed Container Snapshotting
bullets: Designed a distributed snapshotting system for stateful containerized applications, implementing the Chandy-Lamport algorithm to capture globally consistent cuts across nodes communicating over UDP
Built a reliable-UDP sidecar proxy using sequence numbering, buffering, and acknowledgment retransmission to guarantee in-order, exactly-once delivery without modifying application code
